summary_one_line: Input must carry an 'eXIf' chunk-type tag whose 4-byte chunk-length field is 0 or 1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the literal 'eXIf' (so OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_eXIf dispatches) AND whose preceding 4-byte big-endian chunk-length field is < 2 (i.e. 0 or 1), forcing the length<2 TRUE branch at pngrutil.c:2047.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are cmplog/vpc-discovered and reach the blocker at very low wall-clock time (discovered_at=4s), and the per-role overlay marks the length<2 TRUE arm (lines 2048-2052) as winner-only [W]; cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace can splice the logged 'eXIf' chunk-type operand and set the length operand directly so dispatch + length<2 are reached, with the branch hit 29 (vpc) / 7 (cmplog) times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and value_profile blocked in 10/10 trials taking only the FALSE arm (length>=2, the [L] else-branch at 2054+); without I2S substitution they must stumble on both the exact 'eXIf' type tag and a length field below 2 by blind havoc, which never happened in 23+h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the chunk-type comparison against 'eXIf' (and the length comparison), and substitutes the matching operand into the input bytes; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so the only difference between each winner/loser pair is the I2S technique.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the length<2 test in png_handle_eXIf and the globally blocked (winning) side is TRUE.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length < 2)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving arm is the length<2 TRUE branch (lines 2048-2052), exercised only by winner seeds.
Both decisive pairs differ solely by the I2S technique.
naive and value_profile blocked this branch in all 10 trials while their I2S-enabled counterparts resolved it.
Winner seeds reach the branch within seconds of fuzzing start.
The loser arm taken in blocking trials is the length>=2 else-branch (2054 onward), marked loser-only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2047  T=23 F=0  T=0 F=35  if (length < 2)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other, the substitution mechanism enabling the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile have no I2SRandReplace mutator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 4006 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed db598a5afacbd5af` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 4; corroborated with `--fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --seed e947de9b33d09385` showing 2 dash rows at depths 8 and 9. Both decisive winners' ancestor chains contain I2S-floor rows.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an 'eXIf' type tag plus a length<2 field where naive/value_profile resolve the length<2 branch as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), which would mean the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of the chunk-type/length operands.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the I2S advantage primarily comes from splicing the 'eXIf' chunk-type tag versus the length operand itself is not separable from the byte evidence, since the length field is a runtime-parsed chunk header value that may have no stable input-byte pre-image for I2S to target.
